threats.title: Credential brute-force / stuffing
threats.attackVector: Automated submission of common or breached username/password pairs against the login endpoint.

threats.title: Phishing or session fixation
threats.attackVector: Attacker tricks user into authenticating in a session the attacker controls (forged subdomain, fixed session ID).

threats.title: JWT signature stripping / algorithm confusion
threats.attackVector: Forged token with 'alg: none' or HS256/RS256 confusion if server accepts unsigned or weakly-signed tokens.

threats.title: Insufficient login audit trail
threats.attackVector: No structured success/failure logs with IP, UA, geo — disputed access cannot be reconstructed.

threats.title: Username enumeration via error timing or messages
threats.attackVector: Different responses for 'unknown user' vs 'wrong password' (text or response time) leak valid accounts.

threats.title: Account lockout abuse
threats.attackVector: Attacker repeatedly fails logins with valid usernames to lock legitimate users out.

threats.title: Privilege escalation via role claim tampering
threats.attackVector: Client-trusted role claim in JWT or session lets a regular user impersonate admin if not server-verified.

securityRequirements.title: Rate limiting and account lockout
securityRequirements.description: Per-IP and per-account throttling with exponential backoff and CAPTCHA after threshold.

securityRequirements.title: Multi-factor authentication
securityRequirements.description: Require MFA (TOTP, WebAuthn, or push) for privileged accounts and offer it to all users.

securityRequirements.title: Strong password hashing
securityRequirements.description: Argon2id (preferred), bcrypt, or scrypt with per-user salt; never SHA-1/MD5/plaintext.

securityRequirements.title: Secure session tokens
securityRequirements.description: Cryptographically random tokens, rotated on auth, HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite cookies, short TTL.

securityRequirements.title: Authentication event logging
securityRequirements.description: Log success and failure with timestamp, user, IP, UA; ship to immutable audit store.

securityRequirements.title: Generic auth error responses
securityRequirements.description: Identical response body and timing for unknown user vs bad password.

threats.title: Client-only logout (server retains token)
threats.attackVector: App clears local cookie/storage but server keeps JWT/session valid until expiry — stolen tokens stay live.

threats.title: No logout audit event
threats.attackVector: Cannot prove when a session ended for incident reconstruction.

threats.title: Mass session-revocation amplification
threats.attackVector: Forged 'logout-all-devices' calls used to deny service to a target user.

threats.title: CSRF-driven forced logout
threats.attackVector: Attacker page triggers victim's browser to logout, then offers a fake login that captures credentials.

securityRequirements.title: Server-side session invalidation
securityRequirements.description: Delete server-side session row or add token jti to a revocation list on logout.

securityRequirements.title: CSRF protection on logout
securityRequirements.description: Logout must require POST + CSRF token or SameSite=strict cookie.

securityRequirements.title: Logout event logging
securityRequirements.description: Emit audit log on every logout (user, time, IP).

components.label: Forgot Password
components.defaultInputs: email or username, reset token, new password

threats.title: Reset token brute force or guessable tokens
threats.attackVector: Short, sequential, or predictable reset tokens allow attacker to take over accounts.

threats.title: Host-header injection in reset email
threats.attackVector: Attacker manipulates Host header so the reset link points to attacker-controlled domain.

threats.title: Account existence disclosure
threats.attackVector: Different copy for 'we sent an email' vs 'no such user' enumerates valid emails.

threats.title: Email-bombing the user via reset spam
threats.attackVector: Unlimited reset requests flood the victim's inbox and may incur SES costs.

threats.title: Reset bypass to admin accounts
threats.attackVector: Reset flow that doesn't re-verify second factor lets attacker bypass MFA on takeover.

threats.title: No audit trail on password change
threats.attackVector: Cannot prove who initiated/completed the reset; victim cannot dispute the takeover.

threats.title: Weak password accepted on reset
threats.attackVector: Reset flow does not enforce the same password policy as registration.

securityRequirements.title: High-entropy single-use reset token
securityRequirements.description: 128-bit random token, single-use, expires in <=15 minutes, invalidated after success.

securityRequirements.title: Input sanitization on email field
securityRequirements.description: Validate RFC-5322 format and normalize before lookup; reject control chars in headers.

securityRequirements.title: Input validation on new password
securityRequirements.description: Enforce min length 12, breach-list check, and policy parity with registration.

securityRequirements.title: Recycle / history checks
securityRequirements.description: Prevent reuse of last N passwords; force re-auth before allowing reset of MFA.

securityRequirements.title: Strict password policy
securityRequirements.description: Apply same length, complexity, and breached-credential check used at registration.

securityRequirements.title: Allow-list reset link host
securityRequirements.description: Build reset URL from server-side allow-listed origin, never from request Host header.

securityRequirements.title: Audit reset events
securityRequirements.description: Log request, email send, success/failure, and notify the account email of any password change.

threats.title: Bot-driven fake account creation
threats.attackVector: Headless browser farms create fake users for spam, fraud, or resource abuse.

threats.title: Mass-assignment of admin/role fields
threats.attackVector: Client posts extra fields (role=admin, isVerified=true) that ORM blindly assigns.

threats.title: Email enumeration on signup
threats.attackVector: 'Email already in use' message reveals registered accounts.

threats.title: Storage exhaustion via rapid signups
threats.attackVector: Unrate-limited registration fills Users DB and downstream queues.

threats.title: Weak / breached password accepted
threats.attackVector: No length minimum or HIBP check lets users set trivially crackable passwords.

threats.title: Self-promotion to elevated role
threats.attackVector: Default role logic (e.g., first user becomes admin) abused via race or reset.

securityRequirements.title: Bot mitigation
securityRequirements.description: CAPTCHA, proof-of-work, or invisible bot scoring on signup endpoint.

securityRequirements.title: Server-side field allow-list
securityRequirements.description: Bind only explicit fields from request body; never pass req.body straight to ORM.

securityRequirements.title: Strong password policy + breach check
securityRequirements.description: Min 12 chars, allow long passphrases, reject HIBP-known passwords.

securityRequirements.title: Email verification before privileged use
securityRequirements.description: Gate sensitive actions until address ownership is proven via signed link.

securityRequirements.title: Per-IP and per-email rate limit
securityRequirements.description: Throttle signups per IP, ASN, and email domain to slow abuse.

threats.title: Spoofed DB client / shared credentials
threats.attackVector: Shared password reused across services lets a compromised service impersonate another caller.

threats.title: SQL / NoSQL injection
threats.attackVector: Concatenated user input into query string allows arbitrary read/write/admin actions.

threats.title: No row-level audit trail
threats.attackVector: Mutations cannot be attributed to a user/service; deletions cannot be reconstructed.

threats.title: Backups or snapshots leak PII
threats.attackVector: Backups stored unencrypted, in public buckets, or restorable by over-privileged ops accounts.

threats.title: Unbounded query or connection exhaustion
threats.attackVector: Missing LIMIT, no pool cap, or no statement_timeout lets one query starve the cluster.

threats.title: Over-privileged DB user
threats.attackVector: App connects as superuser instead of least-privileged role; SQLi becomes RCE on the DB host.

securityRequirements.title: Parameterized queries / ORM only
securityRequirements.description: No string concatenation; use prepared statements or vetted ORM query builders everywhere.

securityRequirements.title: Least-privilege DB role
securityRequirements.description: Per-service DB user with only required SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE on owned tables. Admin tasks use a separate role.

securityRequirements.title: Encryption at rest
securityRequirements.description: Storage-level (TDE) and application-level encryption for PII columns; managed KMS keys.

securityRequirements.title: TLS for DB connections
securityRequirements.description: Enforce TLS 1.2+ between app and DB; pin server certificate where supported.

securityRequirements.title: Encrypted, access-controlled backups
securityRequirements.description: Encrypted backups, MFA-gated restore, periodic restore tests, retention policy.

securityRequirements.title: DML audit log
securityRequirements.description: Log who/what/when for sensitive tables (users, roles, payments) to immutable store.

threats.title: Unauthenticated cache access
threats.attackVector: Redis/memcached exposed without AUTH or on public network — anyone can read/write keys.

threats.title: Cache poisoning
threats.attackVector: Attacker influences cache keys (e.g. via header manipulation) so other users receive forged data.

threats.title: Sensitive data cached in plaintext
threats.attackVector: Tokens, PII, or session data stored in cache without encryption / appropriate TTL.

threats.title: Cache stampede / unbounded keys
threats.attackVector: Hot key expiry triggers thundering herd; unbounded key creation fills memory.

securityRequirements.title: Authenticated cache + private network
securityRequirements.description: Require AUTH/ACL; bind to private subnet or VPC peer; never expose 6379 publicly.

securityRequirements.title: Server-derived cache keys
securityRequirements.description: Build keys from canonical, server-trusted fields; never echo raw user header values into keys.

securityRequirements.title: Bounded TTL and size for sensitive entries
securityRequirements.description: Short TTL for tokens/PII; eviction policy to cap memory; avoid caching responses with Set-Cookie.

threats.title: Forged payment webhook
threats.attackVector: Attacker POSTs fake 'payment.succeeded' webhooks to mark orders paid without signature verification.

threats.title: Client-controlled amount or currency
threats.attackVector: Browser changes the amount field before submit; server trusts client value.

threats.title: Missing transaction trail
threats.attackVector: Disputes cannot be reconciled because request/response/webhook bodies aren't durably stored.

threats.title: PAN / CVV touches your servers
threats.attackVector: Card data hits your logs or backend instead of going directly to the gateway via tokenization.

threats.title: Payment-API rate limit exceeded by abuse
threats.attackVector: Card-testing fraud floods the gateway, triggering provider rate limits and outages.

threats.title: Refund/void abuse via missing authz
threats.attackVector: Internal endpoints to refund or capture lack role checks; any logged-in user can refund others.

securityRequirements.title: Verify webhook signatures
securityRequirements.description: Verify gateway signature header on every webhook before any state change.

securityRequirements.title: Server-computed amounts
securityRequirements.description: Compute price/tax/fees server-side from product IDs; ignore client-sent totals.

securityRequirements.title: Tokenized card capture
securityRequirements.description: Use gateway-hosted fields / Elements so PAN never touches your server. SAQ-A scope.

securityRequirements.title: Idempotency keys
securityRequirements.description: Send Idempotency-Key on every charge to prevent double-charges on retries.

securityRequirements.title: Immutable transaction log
securityRequirements.description: Append request, response, webhook events to write-once log with at-rest encryption.

securityRequirements.title: Role-gated refund/capture endpoints
securityRequirements.description: Refund, void, capture require explicit role + step-up auth; deny by default.

threats.title: DNS / TLS hijack of vendor host
threats.attackVector: Attacker MITMs the upstream API call without certificate pinning.

threats.title: SSRF via user-supplied URL
threats.attackVector: App fetches a URL the user controls, hitting cloud metadata or internal services.

threats.title: API key leakage in logs / errors
threats.attackVector: Outbound headers logged verbatim, or 5xx response includes Authorization header.

threats.title: Vendor outage cascades
threats.attackVector: No timeout / circuit breaker — slow vendor stalls all worker threads.

securityRequirements.title: Strict TLS for outbound
securityRequirements.description: TLS 1.2+, validate certificate chain, optional pinning for high-trust providers.

securityRequirements.title: SSRF allow-list
securityRequirements.description: Outbound URLs validated against allow-list, deny RFC1918 / metadata IPs and DNS rebinding.

securityRequirements.title: Secret manager for API keys
securityRequirements.description: Keys stored in vault / parameter store; rotated; never in repo or logs.

securityRequirements.title: Timeouts and circuit breakers
securityRequirements.description: Per-request timeout, retry budget, circuit breaker, bulkhead.

threats.title: Missing or weak API authentication
threats.attackVector: Endpoints accept requests without verifying caller identity (no JWT, no mTLS, no API key).

threats.title: Mass assignment / unsafe deserialization
threats.attackVector: Body-binding accepts unintended fields; deserializer instantiates dangerous classes.

threats.title: Verbose errors leak stack/secret
threats.attackVector: 5xx returns stack trace, query, or env vars to caller.

threats.title: IDOR / broken object-level authorization
threats.attackVector: GET /api/orders/{id} returns any order regardless of caller; relies on guessable IDs.

threats.title: No global rate limiting
threats.attackVector: Single client floods the API, exhausting CPU / DB pool.

threats.title: Broken function-level authorization
threats.attackVector: Admin routes guarded only by being unlisted; direct URL grants admin to anyone.

securityRequirements.title: Authenticated by default
securityRequirements.description: Deny-by-default: every route requires authn middleware unless explicitly public.

securityRequirements.title: Object-level authorization checks
securityRequirements.description: Every read/write of a resource verifies the caller owns or is granted access to that exact object.

securityRequirements.title: Schema-validated input
securityRequirements.description: Validate all bodies/queries against a schema (zod, JSON Schema, pydantic); reject extras.

securityRequirements.title: Rate limiting + WAF
securityRequirements.description: Global and per-token rate limits; WAF for OWASP Top-10 patterns.

securityRequirements.title: Generic error responses
securityRequirements.description: Production errors return correlation id only; stack traces only in private logs.

securityRequirements.title: Strict CORS allow-list
securityRequirements.description: Allow-list specific origins; never reflect Origin; never combine '*' with credentials.

threats.title: Unauthenticated producer/consumer
threats.attackVector: Anyone on the network can publish messages or drain queues.

threats.title: Unsigned message payloads
threats.attackVector: Compromised producer injects messages that consumers process as trusted.

threats.title: PII in message bodies / logs
threats.attackVector: Sensitive fields in messages get logged in dead-letter queues or trace exporters.

threats.title: Poison message / unbounded retry
threats.attackVector: A malformed message loops forever, blocks the partition, or fills DLQ.

securityRequirements.title: AuthN/Z for producers and consumers
securityRequirements.description: SASL/mTLS/IAM on the broker; per-topic ACLs; no shared 'guest' creds.

securityRequirements.title: Message integrity
securityRequirements.description: Sign or HMAC payloads; consumer verifies before acting.

securityRequirements.title: DLQ + retry caps
securityRequirements.description: Bounded retry, DLQ, alerting; quarantine poison messages without blocking the partition.

threats.title: Public bucket / world-readable ACL
threats.attackVector: Bucket misconfigured for public list/get; entire dataset indexed by scanners.

threats.title: Path traversal on upload
threats.attackVector: Filename includes '../' segments, overwriting other users' files or system paths.

threats.title: Long-lived signed URLs leaked
threats.attackVector: Signed URL with multi-day TTL ends up in referrer headers, share links, browser history.

threats.title: Unbounded upload size / cost-DoS
threats.attackVector: No size cap; attacker uploads multi-GB files to spike storage and egress bills.

threats.title: Stored XSS / malicious file served
threats.attackVector: User-uploaded HTML/SVG served with text/html on same origin runs attacker JS for victims.

securityRequirements.title: Default-private buckets
securityRequirements.description: Block public access at account + bucket level; deny ACL-based public grants.

securityRequirements.title: Short-lived signed URLs
securityRequirements.description: Signed URLs expire in <=15 minutes; scope to single object and verb.

securityRequirements.title: Validate upload metadata
securityRequirements.description: Server-derived object key, content-type sniffed, size capped, malware scanned.

securityRequirements.title: Serve via separate origin or CDN
securityRequirements.description: User content served from a content-only domain with restrictive CSP and no auth cookies.

threats.title: Cross-site scripting (XSS)
threats.attackVector: Unsanitized user content rendered as HTML/attributes; dangerouslySetInnerHTML on untrusted data.

threats.title: Cross-site request forgery (CSRF)
threats.attackVector: State-changing request triggered by attacker page using victim cookies without CSRF check.

threats.title: Secrets in client bundle
threats.attackVector: API secrets, internal hostnames, or feature flags shipped in the JS bundle.

threats.title: Client-side authorization
threats.attackVector: UI hides admin actions but server doesn't enforce — anyone calling the API directly succeeds.

securityRequirements.title: Strict Content Security Policy
securityRequirements.description: Nonce-based CSP, no 'unsafe-inline'; report-uri for violations.

securityRequirements.title: Output encoding everywhere
securityRequirements.description: Use framework auto-escaping; ban raw HTML insertion of user data.

securityRequirements.title: Secure cookie flags
securityRequirements.description: HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax/Strict on all auth cookies.

securityRequirements.title: All authorization on server
securityRequirements.description: Treat the client as untrusted — never enforce permissions in the browser only.

threats.title: DNS hijack or stale DNS to fake edge
threats.attackVector: Compromised registrar or DNS lets traffic bypass the real CDN/WAF and hit a malicious origin.

threats.title: Cache poisoning at edge
threats.attackVector: Manipulated Vary/Cache-Key or unkeyed inputs cause one user's response to be served to others.

threats.title: Sensitive data cached publicly
threats.attackVector: Cache-Control mis-set on authenticated or tenant-specific JSON; edge caches PII for all visitors.

threats.title: Origin bypass / cache busting flood
threats.attackVector: Attacker crafts requests that always miss cache, overwhelming origin while CDN appears healthy.

threats.title: Misconfigured WAF bypass
threats.attackVector: Overly permissive WAF rules or IP allow-lists let SQLi/XSS reach origin.

securityRequirements.title: TLS 1.2+ and HSTS at edge
securityRequirements.description: Enforce HTTPS, HSTS with preload where appropriate; redirect HTTP to HTTPS.

securityRequirements.title: Never cache authenticated responses by default
securityRequirements.description: Cache-Control: private/no-store for API and tenant-specific JSON unless explicitly designed for shared cache.

securityRequirements.title: Managed WAF + bot rules
securityRequirements.description: OWASP CRS or vendor rules; rate limits and geo blocks as needed.

securityRequirements.title: Origin shield / single egress
securityRequirements.description: Collapse origin connections through shield POP to absorb cache busting.

components.label: Reverse proxy (nginx / origin)
components.defaultInputs: HTTP from CDN, static file requests

threats.title: Header injection / request smuggling
threats.attackVector: Conflicting Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding between proxy and upstream desyncs or smuggles requests.

threats.title: Internal headers leaked to client
threats.attackVector: X-Forwarded-* or internal hostnames reflected in error pages.

threats.title: Slowloris / connection exhaustion
threats.attackVector: Many slow connections tie up worker connections to upstream.

threats.title: Misrouted admin or debug routes
threats.attackVector: /debug or /metrics exposed on same listener as public SPA.

securityRequirements.title: Harden HTTP/2 and request limits
securityRequirements.description: Body size caps, timeouts, disable unsafe HTTP/1.1 downgrade paths per vendor guidance.

securityRequirements.title: Strip and set trusted forwarded headers
securityRequirements.description: Overwrite X-Forwarded-For at edge; do not trust client-supplied internal IPs.

securityRequirements.title: Separate listeners for admin/metrics
securityRequirements.description: Bind metrics and admin to localhost or private network only.

threats.title: Spoofed client identity evades limits
threats.attackVector: Rotating X-Forwarded-For or IPv6 ranges bypass per-IP limits if edge headers are trusted blindly.

threats.title: Counter manipulation in shared store
threats.attackVector: Race or Lua script bug lets attacker reset or inflate another tenant's counters.

threats.title: Redis or store outage denies all traffic
threats.attackVector: Fail-open on limiter lets abuse through; fail-closed becomes self-DoS.

threats.title: 429 responses leak tenant load patterns
threats.attackVector: Different limits per tier reveal which tenant class a key belongs to.

securityRequirements.title: Hierarchical limit keys
securityRequirements.description: Combine tenant id + user id + route; never rely on IP alone for authenticated APIs.

securityRequirements.title: Enforce at edge when possible
securityRequirements.description: Drop abusive traffic before it hits app workers and DB.

securityRequirements.title: Defined fail-open vs fail-closed
securityRequirements.description: Document behavior when Redis is down; prefer bounded fail-closed for auth endpoints.

components.label: JWT & refresh token service
components.defaultOutputs: access JWT, rotated refresh token, revocation events

threats.title: Refresh token theft (XSS, malware)
threats.attackVector: Long-lived refresh token stolen from storage or logs allows full account takeover until rotation.

threats.title: Refresh token replay after rotation
threats.attackVector: Server accepts an old refresh token family after rotation (reuse detection missing).

threats.title: JWT claims leak PII or tenant ids
threats.attackVector: Verbose claims in access tokens cached by intermediaries or logged.

threats.title: Revocation list / Redis hot key
threats.attackVector: Global jti lookup becomes bottleneck; attackers trigger mass revocations.

threats.title: Privilege claims trusted from client
threats.attackVector: role or tenant_id in JWT not re-verified server-side on each sensitive call.

securityRequirements.title: Refresh token rotation + reuse detection
securityRequirements.description: On reuse of an invalidated refresh token, revoke entire token family.

securityRequirements.title: Short access token TTL
securityRequirements.description: Access JWTs minutes-long; sensitive actions may require step-up.

securityRequirements.title: Bind refresh to client where possible
securityRequirements.description: DPoP, mTLS, or rotating device id for high-risk tenants.

securityRequirements.title: Server-side revocation on logout/password change
securityRequirements.description: Invalidate refresh row and publish jti blocklist with bounded TTL.

threats.title: SSRF via tenant URL
threats.attackVector: Tenant registers http://169.254.169.254/ or internal metadata URL; worker fetches it.

threats.title: Webhook signature bypass
threats.attackVector: Receiver skips HMAC verify or compares timing-unsafe; attacker forges events.

threats.title: No idempotent delivery id
threats.attackVector: Receivers cannot dedupe replays; duplicate charges or state corruption.

threats.title: PII in webhook bodies logged
threats.attackVector: Full payload logged to ELK with note content or tokens.

threats.title: Slow tenant endpoint stalls workers
threats.attackVector: No per-tenant concurrency cap; one bad URL blocks all deliveries.

securityRequirements.title: URL validation and SSRF allow-list
securityRequirements.description: HTTPS only, public IP ranges only, block RFC1918 and metadata; optional domain allow-list per tenant.

securityRequirements.title: HMAC-SHA256 with timestamp + idempotency
securityRequirements.description: Sign body + timestamp; reject stale; include event id for dedupe.

securityRequirements.title: Bulkhead workers per tenant
securityRequirements.description: Separate queues and timeouts so one tenant cannot starve others.

threats.title: Forged From / reply-to phishing
threats.attackVector: Misconfigured SPF/DKIM lets attackers spoof your domain in parallel.

threats.title: Header injection via display name
threats.attackVector: Unsanitized name field injects CRLF and Bcc to exfiltrate reset links.

threats.title: Reset / magic links in clear logs
threats.attackVector: Provider webhooks or app logs store full URLs with secrets.

threats.title: Email bombing / quota exhaustion
threats.attackVector: Unthrottled send endpoint spams users or burns provider quota.

securityRequirements.title: Encode headers and ban CRLF in fields
securityRequirements.description: Strict validation on to/from/subject; no raw user strings in headers without encoding.

securityRequirements.title: SPF, DKIM, DMARC aligned
securityRequirements.description: Publish DNS records; monitor DMARC reports.

securityRequirements.title: Per-user and global send limits
securityRequirements.description: Throttle invites and notifications to prevent abuse.

components.label: Public share links
components.defaultOutputs: unguessable URL token, redirect to content

threats.title: Token enumeration / short tokens
threats.attackVector: Predictable or short share ids allow crawling of all shared notes.

threats.title: IDOR on share resource
threats.attackVector: Changing note id in share URL accesses another tenant's note if authz check missing.

threats.title: Cache serves share to wrong user
threats.attackVector: CDN or Redis caches HTML/JSON without Vary on auth or tenant.

threats.title: Share link scraping / hotlink abuse
threats.attackVector: Open graph crawlers hammer origin; no rate limit on public route.

securityRequirements.title: 128+ bit random tokens
securityRequirements.description: Use crypto-secure tokens; store only hash server-side.

securityRequirements.title: Re-verify tenant + object on every fetch
securityRequirements.description: Never trust URL alone; check note belongs to tenant and share is active.

securityRequirements.title: Private cache for shared resources
securityRequirements.description: Cache-Control: private or no-store for tenant-specific share views.

components.label: Multi-tenant isolation
components.defaultInputs: all DB/API requests with tenant context
components.defaultDataStores: Postgres RLS policies, tenant id column

threats.title: Cross-tenant data leak (IDOR)
threats.attackVector: Missing tenant filter in query returns another customer's rows when id is guessed.

threats.title: Tenant id override in request
threats.attackVector: Client sends X-Tenant-Id header that server trusts over JWT tenant.

threats.title: Worker crosses tenant context
threats.attackVector: Async job processes job for tenant A with DB session still bound to tenant B.

threats.title: Logs mix tenant data
threats.attackVector: Structured logs missing tenant_id; support tools expose wrong customer.

securityRequirements.title: Enforce RLS or equivalent in DB
securityRequirements.description: Postgres RLS with session variable or app user per tenant; defense in depth with query filters.

securityRequirements.title: Tenant from signed token only
securityRequirements.description: Derive tenant_id from verified JWT/session; never from client header alone.

securityRequirements.title: Automated cross-tenant tests
securityRequirements.description: Contract tests that A cannot read B's ids across API and workers.

components.label: Presigned URL API (S3 uploads)
components.defaultInputs: file metadata, content-type, tenant, object key

threats.title: Over-broad presigned PUT
threats.attackVector: Policy allows any key prefix or content-type; attacker uploads malware or overwrites others' objects.

threats.title: Long TTL GET URLs leaked
threats.attackVector: GET presigned URL shared in referrer or logs exposes object for hours.

threats.title: Wrong bucket / account in signer
threats.attackVector: Misconfigured role signs into unintended bucket.

threats.title: Unbounded presign requests
threats.attackVector: Attacker mints unlimited PUT URLs and fills storage.

securityRequirements.title: Short TTL and exact object key
securityRequirements.description: Server generates key with tenant prefix; TTL <= 15 minutes; single verb.

securityRequirements.title: Content-Type and size constraints
securityRequirements.description: Bind signature to expected type and max bytes; virus-scan on completion callback.

securityRequirements.title: Per-tenant presign rate limits
securityRequirements.description: Prevent storage cost abuse.

threats.title: Stale tenant / user context in job
threats.attackVector: Global DB connection not reset between jobs causes cross-tenant writes.

threats.title: Poison job payload
threats.attackVector: Attacker enqueues job via bug in producer with SQL in payload.

threats.title: PII in job logs
threats.attackVector: Full note body logged on processing failure.

threats.title: Job backlog exhausts workers
threats.attackVector: No max concurrency or DLQ; one tenant floods queue.

securityRequirements.title: Explicit tenant context per job
securityRequirements.description: Pass tenant_id in payload; set session variable at job start; verify before writes.

securityRequirements.title: Validate job payloads
securityRequirements.description: Schema-versioned messages; reject unknown fields.

securityRequirements.title: Per-tenant concurrency caps
securityRequirements.description: Fair scheduling so one tenant cannot starve workers.
